reviews.review_text:
The story is great.
The gameplay is very engaging.
I really regret not playing it sooner.
This game is an ADHD Gamers Dream. So much variety and tributes to all types of game genres

A surprisingly rich experience! I was expecting a casual diving-fishing game with a side of restaurant management sim, but there was quite a lot more to see. 
Following the story was fun. The characters, style, and frequent animated scenes are very charming and full of personality, some even getting a chuckle out of me with their silliness. 
I also didn’t expect to enjoy the soundtracks of some of the diving areas that much (Glacier Zone my beloved…)

Gameplay stays engaging despite the repetitive loop with each dives being different either looking for specific species of fish or items, as there is quite a decent number of side quests to do along the way. Diving itself was rather tricky and frustrating at times, with the penalty of loosing all your inventory but ONE item in case of failure being quite harsh. Sometimes it felt like a stealth game trying to get past overzealous sharks praying to stay unnoticed, but I guess it added a sense of challenge. 

For those who like the genre, I think it’s definitely a must play.

After finishing and getting the platinum for Dave The Diver it ended up being one of the most charming and genuinely fun games I’ve played in a long time. The game never takes itself too seriously, and that’s honestly its biggest strength, just when you settle into the gameplay loop, it throws some absurdly hilarious cutscene, random mini game, or over the top moment at you that somehow only makes the experience better. What surprised me most is how flexible the game is: you can focus almost entirely on diving, running the sushi restaurant, farming fish, completing side quests, taking photos, or cleaning the ocean floor, and each part feels satisfying enough to carry the game on its own. The characters are lovable, the animated scenes are packed with personality, and the atmosphere is fantastic. Despite the repetitive structure, the gameplay stays consistently engaging because every dive feels different depending on what fish, upgrades, or objectives you’re chasing. The diving can definitely get tense and frustrating at times; losing almost your entire inventory after dying is brutal, and sneaking past aggressive sharks sometimes feels like a stealth game, but that challenge adds real excitement and makes successful dives feel rewarding. 

Overall, Dave the Diver constantly balances humor, charm, challenge, and variety in a way that makes it incredibly hard to put down. 8.5/10

I wanted to love this game, and it's quite cozy. If you just want to grind the core gameplay loop it will let you. But the rewards track suffers diminishing returns fast.
None of the harpoon mechanics feel very good. The sea people are insufferable and their fetch quests are intolerable. Restaurant management is the best element in my opinion, but there's not enough. Fish farming seems cool but when it can be unlocked there's already a lot of burnout suffered (especially if you get soft-locked at pulling out stakes and need to restart).
The music is great for the first 30mins but the same track playing every time you enter the water or manage the restaurant gets very very old. The jukebox functionality could be vastly more functional, meaning it could actually play the track you select while you play the game itself.
I may finish it at some point much later but overall a bit dissatisfying, after a compelling start.

DLC Achievements: None, as of yet. 
Missable Achievements: 1 achievement is missable. "Ration Eater". It's rather hard to miss, though. 

This game is incredibly fun. It oozes charm. It's addicting. It's challenging without being frustrating. It's easygoing without being boring. The graphics are incredible. The characters are loveable. It always felt like there was something more for me to do, but not in an overwhelming way. I truly loved every minute of playing it. 

I would say that if you're only buying this game for the roguelite tag, you might want to reconsider. There is some elements of a roguelite here, but I felt like the randomized elements were pretty lacking. Each area only had a few variants it would cycle through. Some didn't vary at all. Loot would vary, but not in any meaningful way. I don't mean this as a slight to the game. Each map feels beautifully crafted. I think procedural generation would've diminished from that. Still, though, it bears mentioning. 

It also bears mentioning that the movement speed is quite slow. It didn't feel so bad when I started playing, but towards the end of my playtime I found it frustrating. Some areas are large and open and so, so slow to traverse. And you'll have to do it over and over again. 

I found the endgame pretty lacking. Eventually, most ingredients are easily farmable. I stopped having a reason to dive-- which sucked because that was my favorite part of the game. 

I don't mean to keep harping on the game's negative aspects. This is such an easy game to recommend that I felt I had to bring up something wrong with it. Honestly, the positives far outweigh the negatives. It is an excellent game and a truly memorable experience. I highly recommend it.

[b]Dave the Diver[/b] is one of the best "casual" games out there!

You play as [b]Dave the Diver[/b]... - *badum tzz* - and dive... obviously :D
The gameplay is a satisfying loop: you dive in the morning & afternoon into a beautiful blue hole, which gets deeper and deeper as you progress, catching all kinds of fish and then using your catches to run a sushi bar in the evening. During the course of the game, you gain access to all kinds of catching mechanics: different harpoons, knives, traps, guns, nets, bombs, etc. This loop, as well as the catching mechanics themselves, makes this a perfect casual game as well a game with great depth for people searching for that.

By collecting different kinds of parts and ingredients, you can upgrade your equipment and refine your sushi dishes! There are also many "boss" fish, like sharks or whales, each with its own behaviors and mechanics.

Later on, between dives, you also have to manage your vegetable and chicken farm, as well as your fish farm! The developers integrated many entertaining - and sometimes really innovative - mini-games into the gameplay.

The art style is lovely pixel art mixed with over-the-top humor. The cutscenes actually made me laugh a few times, as the game doesn't take itself too seriously. Some of the deeper & darker areas can even be a little bit creepy...

The storytelling, in my opinion, is phenomenal. The game starts off quite casual & relaxing but then literally pulls you deeper & deeper into a thrilling as well as entertaining story about saving the Blue Hole, with a clear focus on environmental awareness and xenophobia. It can absolutely be enjoyed without looking too deeply into it - *badum tzz* - or having to grind, but if you feel like doing that, it's absolutely possible (like collecting every dish, every fish, or every mini-game).

Overall, it's truly a one-of-a-kind game that surprised me every few hours! Totally worth the price tag and it also features two small free DLCs!

[u]8,5/10[/u]

[h1]Final Score: [b]77/100[/b][/h1]

Dave the Diver is the type of game that hooks you early, reels you in slowly, and then keeps revealing more systems long after you think you understand what it is. What starts as a simple game of diving, catching fish, and running a sushi restaurant turns into a surprisingly packed game with upgrades, staff management, farming, side quests, boss fights, collections, events, and more content than expected.

The gameplay is addictive, satisfying, and easy to return to, though the game can start to feel overloaded as more systems are introduced. The humour is strong point. Its exaggerated characters and playful stereotypes in a way that feels more like older games: less sanitized, less agenda-driven, and more focused on actually being funny for the audience playing it. Duff is a amazing, and the cutscenes give the game a lot of personality.

The presentation is not perfect. The background music can get repetitive, and some systems feel a bit clunky, but the overall game is far stronger than expected. For the price, Dave the Diver delivers a huge amount of content, a relaxing grind, and a genuinely good time. Recommended.

[h2]Score Breakdown[/h2]
[b]Gameplay Experience:[/b] 20/25
[b]Presentation & Immersion:[/b] 14/25
[b]Design & Technical Aspects:[/b] 20/25
[b]Final Considerations:[/b] 23/25

[h2]Think I got it wrong? Generate your own review at https://playeropinion.com[/h2]

A very shallow game about deep-sea diving. It's not a horrible time, the writing is semi humorous but the same jokes get tiring after a while. The gameplay is a collection of minigames half of which you'd rather just ignore. And it's a "roguelike" where the level is 95% the same every run so all they achieve by being "roguelike" is artificially lengthening the game by getting you to re-tread the same waters at the start of each session.

All in all I finished the game and didn't suffer doing it, but it's not something I can recommend.

The game has a great premise, but throws too many unrelated ideas and mechanics at you, making the game loop way too crowded after a few hours of playing. Each day takes forever to finish and you are pulled in 6 different directions by sidequests. And the cutscenes just do not end.
The creators clearly had fun making this game, which shows from the humor, all the unique characters, and all the different mechanics and plotlines thrown in, which is why I really wanted to like it. If they make another game I'll still give it a chance.

An enormously fun little game about .... scuba diving. Diving, hunting fish, and running a Sushi restaurant. It uses a beautiful retro pixilated art style. There is a story to progress, but most of all, I just love the days of quietly slipping into the sea and exploring the depths. 

At night, the restaurant is open, providing an entirely different kind of game play that can be at times, frustrating, but at times surprisingly rewarding. And regardless of what else, it helps provide a counter point to the diving you do during the day, giving the game "greater depth". 

Fun game, highly recommended.

Peak game.

I love the big variety of game mechanics here, some characters, beautiful graphics and the atmosphere that this game has. Plus, I've never been so invested in getting all of the achievements like I was in "Dave the diver" :D

Sure, some parts were frustrating and confusing, but it's forgivable as there are only a few of such moments in the entire game, which is very long.

I would recommend playing "Dave the diver" to people who like casual  fishing games with quirky characters and fun story to follow.

So before I leave my review let me just say, I am not the biggest gamer, sometimes I can play for a hour or two and other times I will turn my PC on, look at my games, and then turn it of again.

HOWEVER !

This was not the case with Dave the Diver, from the moment of installation I was harpooned and reeled in, from the various stunning ocean backdrops, vast amount of fish you can catch, boss battles, the sushi restaurant, story line, and the list goes on, Dave the Diver definitely ticked all my boxes. 

I am super excited to see the continued developement of the game with the upcoming "In The Jungle" DLC and hopefully it won't stop there.

If you haven't played it, I would highly recomend giving it a try.
Fun game, I like that there's so many things to collect and that it switches between diving in the ocean and managing the restaurant. The story is interesting with a bit of humour.
I beat the game three years ago but I still hop on every so often to dive and make sushi. I absolutely love everything about this game and am stoked for the DLC.

Dave The Diver was a great game to sit down and rewind after a tough day with. There's tons to do and tons to collect, the game can never truly be found boring in my opinion. If you've found yourself ever considering  to playing, I'd suggest you give it a try, its more fun then you'll ever know.
Incredibly made, extremely polished, funny, and the story was super fun.
This is a very addictive game. The objective and the wonderful underwater world will keep you hooked. My favourite are the cut scenes of Bancho

This is probably the very few games that I give recommend at the beginning but eventually choose not recommending it. To be honest catching fish and marketing simulation part is totally okey really intriguing, discovering new regions, new fishes, new weapons and new food were really fantastic but after 10 hours---after discovering sea people village the game becomes a waste time, lots of design of the missions were totally meaningless, the dining centre and the guardians are only 10 meters away from each other why do I need to pass them the lunch. The puzzle design in the later half of this game was even an upgrade version of wasting time, they are not letting me feel accomplishment but torturing. My strongest desire is to catch fish this is the reason I buy this game, I push the main story line only to discover new regions and new fishes but when I got a bulk of fishes in my package and found that I have to do a lot of meaningless puzzles in order to reach new regions, dude that moment is really disappointing.
A lot of fun, and a suprising amount of depth.

Its like a addiction, "Gotta catch 'em all!" I meant sleep~ 
10/10 Funny animation cut scene. We need more games like this. 
Compare this small indie game to AAA games, the AAA games are bad jokes.

# Dave the Diver Review – A Masterclass in Fun, Variety, and Rewarding Gameplay

*Dave the Diver* is one of those rare games that constantly surprises you while never losing sight of what makes it fun. What begins as a simple adventure about diving for fish and running a sushi restaurant quickly evolves into an unforgettable experience packed with exploration, management, storytelling, humor, and an endless stream of fresh ideas.

The core gameplay loop is brilliantly designed. During the day, you explore the mysterious Blue Hole, discovering new species, gathering resources, and uncovering secrets hidden beneath the ocean's surface. At night, you transform your catch into a thriving sushi business. The balance between underwater exploration and restaurant management creates a satisfying rhythm that keeps every session engaging.

One of the game's greatest strengths is its reward system. Every dive feels meaningful because your efforts directly contribute to upgrading equipment, unlocking recipes, expanding your restaurant, and progressing the story. There is always something new to work toward, and the game consistently rewards curiosity and experimentation. Instead of becoming repetitive, it continually introduces new mechanics, tools, quests, and mini-games that make the experience feel fresh even dozens of hours in.

The story is equally impressive. What initially appears to be a lighthearted fishing adventure gradually reveals a surprisingly rich narrative filled with memorable characters, emotional moments, humor, and unexpected twists. The game blends comedy and adventure effortlessly, creating a world that feels both charming and alive.

Visually, the pixel art is stunning. Every underwater biome is packed with detail, colorful marine life, and smooth animations that bring the world to life. Combined with an excellent soundtrack and expressive character animations, the presentation gives the game a unique personality that stands out in a crowded gaming landscape.

What truly elevates *Dave the Diver* is its ability to constantly surprise the player. Critics and players alike have praised how the game introduces new ideas throughout its entire runtime without overwhelming the experience. Whether it's boss battles, special events, side activities, or unexpected story developments, the game always finds ways to keep things exciting.

It's easy to see why the game earned widespread critical acclaim, including a Metacritic score of 90 and praise from major outlets for its creativity, addictive gameplay loop, and exceptional variety. Critics described it as "engaging from start to finish," while many players highlighted how difficult it was to put down once they started playing.

Overall, *Dave the Diver* is a near-perfect blend of exploration, progression, management, and storytelling. It delivers a rewarding gameplay experience, a fantastic cast of characters, and an incredible sense of adventure. Whether you're diving into the depths for treasure, managing a bustling sushi restaurant, or uncovering the mysteries hidden beneath the sea, every moment feels purposeful and enjoyable.

**Rating: 10/10 – An unforgettable adventure that continually rewards players with creativity, charm, and one of the most satisfying gameplay loops in modern gaming.**

This is an incredible game, especially for the Steam Deck!
The story is very well written, gameplay itself is thought out with a lot of possibilities without overwhelming the player. The progression is linear, that means a new element of feature gets added gradually, so learning new stuff was so natural. 
The animations and dialogues were wholesome and sometimes hilarious. 
Go and play it!
Looked for a cozy DREDGE-like fishing game, ended up committing unspeakable acts against the ecosystem and doing increasingly unhinged tasks. 11/10 would play again.
Gameplay loop is just fantastic. There's nothing else to really say, you should buy this game.

this game has easilyyyy became one of my favorite games. the pixelated art style caught my eye originally, but the content of the game definitely kept me. i will forever recommend this game to people who want a “cozy” wind down game to play.
PERFECT GAME and relaxing good colors in it and perfect sushi

This game is an excellent and holistic experience. Great mechanics underwater, movement never feels sluggish. The land-game is also highly rewarding. Running the Sushi shop for Bancho is a pleasure, and new mechanics are introduced at a steady, breezy pace, usually with a new character or two. Check this ♥♥♥♥ out, the coming update ought to be good, looks like they're adding a whole new game to the existing one. You'll be sure to have many long hours of enjoyment with this title babes.

Explore the sea, manage your restaurant, defeat small fish and big fish, help people, collect things.
It's a mixture of a exploration game with a restaurant management game, with a RPG game and some others.

[b] One of the things I have to highlight the most is that you can tell this game was made with a lot of love. Nothing was rushed or scuffed. The details (and exaggerated cutscenes) are incredible. Hello, game companies, learn from this one [/b]

[h3] Pros [/h3]
- Great controls
- Combat is pretty good, though I wish melee was more viable somehow
- Awesome progression, you never stop unlocking new stuff until the very end of the game (lots of games don't get progression as good as this one)
- Upgrades to your stuff actually feel satisfying (also another thing games don't usually get it right)
- There is always stuff happening, this makes the game not feel boring at any point. There is A LOT of content
- Main quest line and all side quests length are just right, doesn't feel like a short game nor a very long one (took me 42 hours for main quests, side quests and a few optional things with maybe a few hours being afk). I optimized all objectives during my dives but it could have easily have taken 50% more time to finish everything if I took my time
- Visual clarity, it's easy to differentiate everything in the sea, except for maybe nettable creatures
- Great soundtrack
- Great graphics (for a 2D semi pixelated game)
- Achievements are not a super pain to get, though I'll most likely not aim for 100%
- Fun and very elaborated minigames, honestly I'd play some of these on my phone if I could
- The game didn't feel grindy at any point, I never really had to do dives for grinding resources though I tried to min max and optimize my dives as much as I could

[h3] Neutral [/h3]
- Story is ok, nothing spectacular, pretty wholesome I'd say
- [spoiler] Beluga in Sea People city has no reason to be paid, though the currency used shouldn't be a concern after the very beginning [/spoiler]. Not a big deal but I don't understand the reasoning here, especially because you can afford a slightly cheaper price in certain areas
- I wish some puzzles were difficult, they were all very easy and some of them were even impossible to get it wrong
- At some point, we have plenty of things to manage if we want to make use of everything, it feels like chores if you do it too much. I completed the game about the same time I was getting tired of doing all the stuff, so it's ok. And no, [spoiler] I'm not paying that much money to have the guy take care of my farm, it's just not worth it until I'm making like 10k+ profit every day and have no other use for money [/spoiler]. I'd honestly play more to farm at least the achievements but managing everything kinda threw me off
- The game has a more casual vibe, it doesn't really feel hard or challenging at any point

[h3] Cons [/h3]
- Lack of QoL in restaurant management. I don't know how many plates I served each night to have some sort of idea of how much food should be in the menu. I'd love to min max without any waste of materials I'll have to grind back if needed
- Lack of QoL in [spoiler] fish tanks [/spoiler], let me preset things to either [spoiler] sell or send to kitchen [/spoiler] automatically so I don't have to [spoiler] visit the tank [/spoiler] every day and have one more thing to manage every day
- [spoiler] Additional branch [/spoiler] felt unnecessary and meaningless. I could have missed something because I finished the game right when I unlocked it, so it didn't actually [spoiler] 
 worked a single day [/spoiler] 
- Workers progression is not as good as all the other things, they should probably have half the levels that they have (from 20 cap to 10)
- The game never seemed to inform me what is Procure and Appeal in workers stats. I assumed Procure is the ability to find items when you dispatch them? But I'm not sure
- I never felt like I needed all the different weapons the game have to offer, it feels like I only need a high DPS weapon and one weapon to get Rank 3 fishes which is not even a necessity tbh. I could see uses for the options of weapons but it never felt that one option was better than another in any situation, it always came down to dealing a lot of damage with a good proportion of damage to bullets ratio or just the ability to capture fish without killing them. No use for AoE weapons, no real use for [spoiler] poison, fire, lightning, [/spoiler] etc

Do NOT let the premise fool you.
"Diving game meets sushi restaurant sim" sounds like a fever dream, but Dave the Diver pulls it off with so much charm and confidence that you'll forget to question it. The ocean is gorgeous and genuinely unpredictable, the restaurant grind is oddly satisfying, and the story goes places you'd never expect.
It's funny, it's chill, it's occasionally tense, and it never runs out of ideas.
10/10 Recommend. Just don't start a dive when you have somewhere to be.

The theme is great. The story is great. The art is great. Playing it feels really relaxing. Sometimes I even forget that this game has boss battles from the FC era and think of it as a full-on simulation management game. While promoting ecological protection, it also conveys the idea of humans coexisting with nature through running a restaurant. Really nice.

It's outrageous that I haven't written a review for this game sooner. I've played this game since it first released and I absolutely love it even up to now.

The amount of love the fans and developers alike pour into this game really tops it all off. The content that keeps coming after all these years, the story that's amusing, the game mechanics that you can never get tired of - this game got me to love pixelated graphics again. Can't wait for the next DLC coming out!
Beautiful pixel art and an engaging gameplay loop, though not infinitely engaging, to me, like Stardew is.
Such a great and beautiful game. Would recommend!
Game is good until it isn't. They try to throw too much at you when I just want to explore and catch fish.
It is very cozy and nice, but sometimes stressful

Eu amei o jogo. Tem de tudo um pouco: exploração, gerenciamento de restaurante e varios outros minijogos que fazem a gameplay ser unica e se renovar durante as horas jogando. Não é um jogo dificil, mas isso não significa que seja menos divertido ou que tenha menos coisas para fazer. Fiz todos os achievements em 40 horas, mas poderia continuar jogando por muito mais tempo, já que ainda tem mais conteúdo para ser feito. Estou aguardando ansiosa pela DLC :)
Fun gameplay loop, but kinda loses steam in the middle
The game has a big amount of story line, it is a game with bosses and missions and more it takes a lot of skill t5o get the hang of it. I recommend this game for 8+ year olds.

I'm not very good at giving reviews but after playing this I must say this game is absolutely worth playing the pixel and 3d animation fits so well, the characters are so memorable and funny with great references, gameplay is so thrilling from fishing, fighting, cooking, to serving customers and wonderful story I have to say I'm hooked so if you want a marine fishing exploration with some fun moments then this game is the one also look forward for the jungle expansion!

This game is a truly delightful expirience. its funny amd challenging, and the chracters has so much personality. the story is unique and interesting and is just emotional enough to make you feel bad without taking itself too seriously!

there is just one [i] tiny [/i] little problem
(emphasis on tiny... and welcome to the nitpick btw)

Some of the fish are so gosh dang small that you don't even realise its a catchable fish! More then once I have swam by one super rare fish that I needed to complete thta section because I thought it was part of the background or foreground details. I know this is such a small thing, but as someone with poor eyesight I wish they would have an accesibility feature that highlights the fish. Some sort of halo you could toggle so you dont swim past the tiny ones?

Other then that though I HIGHLY reccomend this game! It is worth every penny!

This game starts off well enough but it quickly descends into one half-baked mini game after another. I'm not even sure what the actual game is since one minute your diving and searching, the next your throw into a stealth game where you're hiding behind boxes in a factory. It makes no sense what so ever. It feels like the developers had no clue what kind of game they wanted to make so they just pieced a bunch of completely unrelated gameplay together and made up an absurd story to stick it together. This could have easily been GOTY material if it wasn't for the random changes in gameplay mechanics from one moment to the next. The game is kind of a nightmare to play with a controller. For some things, you'll probably want to have a mouse on hand or it's going to get very frustrating. I do recommend the game but it could/should have been SO much better.

Cute spin on a stardew valley play pattern. Diving for fish, cooking with Bosco, and serving mxmtoon really hits the spot for winding down with a feels good game. Lovable characters and world, every part of the game is fun.
I could've technically play this for free on amazon luna since I have prime, but once i tried it out I fell in love with it so hard I NEEDED to give the creator $20 for it.
Originally played this with PS Plus and loved it a lot. Just a really fun and addictive gameplay loop
absolutely LOVE the gameplay, just got an issue with the anti-shark-propaganda

It's a great game that provides multiple avenues on the way you want to play it. You want to only do the main quests? Sure go ahead. If you want a chill cooking/kitchen sim at times, you have that as well. Looking forward to the Jungle DLC!

One of the best indie games and dare i say best games ive ever played in general. The story, the gameplay, everything is just fantastic. Although i beat it on Playstation im still yet to 100% the game on steam and will honestly come back to it soon. I have also seen that the dlc "In the jungle" is soon to release and the prequel game "Bancho the chef" has also been announced. Can't wait. Absolute masterpiece.

This is a super fun game. Collecting and catching different kinds of fish is highly entertaining, and upgrading my gear to unlock new areas gives me a lot of motivation to keep playing. However, I absolutely hate the Sea People Village quests. They are incredibly boring and tedious. The card game quests in particular are just terrible (I don't play card games at all). The Sea People Village quests are honestly so mind-numbing, but I have to bite the bullet and grind through them just to access new areas. Overall, I love the daily fishing, but I despise all the quests in the Sea People Village.
Pretty fun game. It's simple yet engaging. I love the art design.
This game is so fun and addicting I did not think I would like it but i really got into it!

This could quite possibly be one of my favorite games to play during the time i'm at home after a stressful day. The beautiful scenery, the "mostly" chill atmosphere, the incredible soundtrack, I love it all! Even looking at all the food in the game, It's actually making me consider eating seafood, something that i usual never eaten before. Overall, a really excellent game to play!

Its a fun and chill game. I love relaxing and just catching fish. My only gripe is the pacing is a bit too much. Sometimes events and objective are back-to-back or even overlap and I just want to go fishing. In a single day I fought 2 boss fish, completed a storyline quest, took 2 photos, unlocked an entirely new area, opened a branch, beat an Italian cook-off, and got a new app game on the phone (On TOP of Diving, Farming, and fishing) One thing is sure, You will never be bored with this game.

How best to describe the game? You are an obese Athlete, a so called diver. You need to annhilate opposing enemy fish with a device that they call harpoon. Annihilated fish is then repurposed by some kind of a non-asian ninja into food that you need to offer to other humans. 
Your whale-like body attracts dolphins. they are your friends. you do not shoot friends with a harpoon. only the other fish. 
I am about to sort of understand the game, I think. It is a slow process for sure.
in a time of great suffering and pain, where most games devolve into sweat fueled shouting and rage, there is a comfort to know there is a nice and peaceful game where you hunt fish and serve sushi.

awesome game. everything is fun. everything feels good. it tickles different kinds of tisms depending on which part of the game you're currently engaging with. seriously good experience. only caveat i personally experienced is controller issues on the mac version but that could just as easily be the fault of mac os.

As you’ll notice quickly in Dave the Diver, the artists did a fantastic job. The blend of 3D models and 2D sprites make for great visuals throughout the game. The pixel art character animations in cutscenes were impressive and endearing. There are many different sea creatures and environments to discover, along with different methods to catch certain types of sea creatures. The sushi restaurant gives players a means to earn money to then get upgrades for diving gear, as well as breaking up the diving with tycoon gameplay.

Though there’s a lot to love, I personally felt Dave the Diver has some pretty glaring issues. The hurtboxes on the fish are comically bad. You can shoot a harpoon straight through the entire torso of some of the largest fish in the game, without it registering as a hit. Another issue is that Dave is quite slow, even while using two mobility charms. If the game was shorter, this would be less grating, but you need to swim through the same areas many, many times to progress. Finally, the game frequently forces you to wait for in-game days after completing one task before you can continue to the next. Combine this with Dave's speed, and it just feels like the game is padding its runtime.

I don’t think Dave the Diver lives up to the hype that surrounded it, but I do think it’s a good game overall. If you’re a fan of creature capture games and can forgive its flaws, it might be worth picking up. 6.5/10

The visuals are beautiful, and the gameplay loop is simple but surprisingly addictive. It can get repetitive after a while, yet it had me playing for 10 hours straight when I first downloaded it. My interest faded quickly afterward, but I still had a great time with it.
Absolutely wonderful game, I cannot wait for the new DLC! Love the art style <3

Dave the Diver is a simple yet engaging game. Gameplay is split into two parts, the diving and restaurant management halves, and both are well done.

The diving part is the more interesting part, essentially you explore the depths, catching fish and having adventures. There's things you need to find in order to fulfill certain missions, and other things you need to retrieve to participate in research, that helps fund your activities. Money is used to upgrade your equipment and weapons, the latter of which you'll need in order to be able to survive the more aggressive creatures underwater. Of course fishing is very important if you want to do the following properly.

Running the restaurant. You also have a sushi restaurant, and it's here you get your money to be able to upgrade your equipment and explore new areas. It's relatively simple, you need to serve the sushi and drinks to customers in time. You hire people to make sushi and help you serve the customers, as quickly it becomes impossible to do the work by yourself. You have to also invest in training your staff, upgrading your sushi recipes (and what you can make is determined by which fish you bring back from your dives), and researching new recipes that bring better profits.

It's quite fun, it doesn't pressure you to do things immediately, you can go at your own pace. The only time things are on a timer is certain events where <theme of fish> is needed on <date>, so you need to catch a decent amount of them and make them dominant in your menu so that you can get more money into the restaurant. You could ignore those events if you want, though it is more fun to go along with it. Even when you have a customer who is "I want <special dish>", they simply hang around until you get around to finding the ingredients to make it. It makes things somewhat chill. The need to prioritise between paying for better equipment and investing in the restaurant forces you to make choices, however it's not a big issue, making money to pay for things is relatively simple so if you can't afford an upgrade you'll be able to in a few in game days. No problem,.

So yeah, good game.

Dave the Diver sure is a unique game. The characters are lovable, the storyline is engaging - despite being very cheesy at times -, and the gameplay is friendly to new and experienced gamers. It's a longer game than I'm used to, but it really progresses at the player's pace. Could I have completed it quicker...likely, but I enjoyed the freedom of not being rushed from task to task. The game's pacing felt very organic and player-driven. I was surprised by how many mechanics were present in the game; it felt like they were never-ending, which was a bit annoying at times. It was likely to keep engagement, but having new game mechanics introduced partway and late in the game felt like the developers were throwing pasta at the wall to see what stuck. Like, the gameplay was a little less established and cohesive. Overall, I really loved playing Dave and will be playing all the DLCs. It's a solid game, and I highly recommend it for anyone who's looking for something fun and quirky.

Really fun indie game about a dude who goes underwater to collect fish and sustain his Sushi restaurant out somewhere in a tropical/resort-like setting. There are subtle themes of Japanese culture, and i think its a pretty good game to play if you want some kind of Stardew Valley-est vibe where you can turn your brain off for some parts and chill.

An incredibly lovable game made by people who clearly love games. If I could only recommend one game for everyone to play, it would be this. Never met a game that is as sweet, earnest, and delightful as it is addicting.
This game is surprisingly fun to play and for some reason I constantly find myself logging back in to play again.

The main story is engaging, the difficulty of boss fights are well adjusted. Sharks were really terrifying for me in the beginning. As the game progresses, Dave got better equipments and become more capable of fighting hostile fish, although some of them still feels annoying to see. The overall gameplay experience is rewarding, which keeps me coming back to this game from time to time. 

And I really enjoyed its collaboration with Balatro, it is fun to play a simpler version of a game I like, inside another game I like. 

I highly recommend this game to people who likes pixel graphic, enjoys adventure and managing a restaurant. Looking forward to more DLC updates and collaborations with other games!
The core gameplay is so incredibly fun, that I'm suffering through all the painfully annoying side quests just to play it
This game is a driving factor in me purchasing an AYN Thor. This $10 game has essentially committed me to a $400 console and I don't regret it at all.

it holds the same aspects of a hunting game with the animals that attack you if you get too close, such as the shark, lionfish, titan triggerfish, and so on, but also the aspects of a relaxing game with catching the other fish. as well of these, I like the idea of not having a health bar, but the oxygen you have is the your health. all in all, it holds many aspects for many different playing styles.
Good game, only 14 hours in the but diving is already getting a little repetitive. Every other aspect of the game is super fun and original

This game is amazing and addictive. It is very fun and one of my favorite games in awhile. 

My only critique is that the cut scenes feel forced upon me. They're very back to back and there is no way to control some of the sequences of the storyline's pacing. With all the different characters' side stories, it is overwhelming to receive them one after another.

Like if Subnautica ate an edible.

Those of us that have grown up with all of these 100+ hour games need games like this in our old age.

Our backs are sore, hair is graying and we are not sure what video games are becoming but the last few years have shown they are becoming something a little worse than they were in my hey day.

Unless you count all these little throwbacks, this game feels like it was ripped out of 2006 and optimized for today.

Play for 20 minutes or 2 hours and it should not feel like you dive into the same water twice, it is really neat the elements added and just makes for an all around fun and relaxing experience.
A very charming game. Starts out simple but gets INSANE.
At fist.. I don't really expecting anything good. But I am very wrong, it is so addictive. The story line is kind of attractive and the pixelated rendering is quite cute.
my chill zone, absolutely love the art, the vibes, the music, snatched it on the discount, woth worth it
This game has so much to do. Just when you think you'll get bored, something new pops up. I really recommend this game to everyone. Very fun and soothing.
The game is the epitome of the genre "casual." No other game has been more casual.
Very relaxing and loads of fun, would definitely recommend.
Played this game with 0 expectations and very much surprised me. I do think if you want to keep playing for a long time the DLC's packages are a must. Played mostly on my steam deck.
One of the most well made games in the history of games. The attention to detail matched with never running out of things to accomplish is one of a kind.

This game keeps the excitement alive with new things and tasks every so often. And it is so relaxing to play. The music, the animation and the videos that come up every so often are so enjoyable. Highly recommend playing this with a controller rather than a keyboard.
Fun little game. For the size and asking price of the game, it can get pretty deep(pun intended). I play this on a Rog Xbox ally, the base model and it runs perfectly with full controller support.

This game is certainly one of the most memorable indies I've played in recent memory. There is a certain addicting drive that wants you to see Dave succeed with his sushi business. I do wish that there was a means to automate certain things (handling fish tanks, a manager for the main sushi bar, etc.) as the game does have a lot to offer in terms of things to do for a daily routine.

So much to do and I'm only 9 hours in, very addicting. Definitely a little cheesy in it's uber-good-natured way, that Japanese style of story and prose, but totally fine. Can easily play with music or something and skim dialogue, etc. Awesome game.

The game is great, BUT

I had tons of fun on this game, i played nonestop but after a certain number of hours it ends up being a giant fetch-quest game. and it unfortunately, for me, became insufferable. I dont believe I finished the game fully. I wish there was about 50% more of the sushi bar, because you spend 2 or 3 dives (been awhile) and all this time collecting fish and doing quests, to spend 3 minutes reaping the benefits of your hard work diving. 6.5/10. Definately play it, but at some point you MAY run into an emotional wall like I did.

Don't usually play games that dont involve fps shooting but this game is insanely addictive and fun the loop is pretty solid im in the first area for 10 hours fishing and managing the shop, highly recommend.

Super cool. Love it. 
There's no other game like dave the diver. "Cozy game" is an extremely broad category that it fits in. Its and adventure, and an awesome story. There's awesome free dlc, and they still are coming out with more dlc that I'm excited about. The worst thing about dave the diver is that I'll never be able to play it again for the first time.
i love this game. so funny. endless content, constantly pleasantly surprised by new mini game scenarios and plot is awesome.
Love this game, Played it a while upon release, but now I'm back to play it on Steam deck. perfect game to play while sitting on the couch with wife watching god knows what.
Awesome game. The progression is fair it keeps you playing and the story elements are fantastic.
The group info only accepts messages from people in its organization or on its allowed senders list, and your email address isn't on the list.
very very good game, i trully love the art style, the comedy, and the gameplay

This is one of my all time favorites. The over the top anime humor, the restaurant loop that doesn't over stay its welcome and the diving that takes up as much time as you let it. Just try it if you are at all interested. Refund it if it doesn't grab you in the first 2 hours. 

FYI the missions or quests cannot be failed. If it is on a timer then it will come back around. If it doesn't have a timer it will sit for as long as you want. So play at your own pace. Don't feel any need to rush. Honestly slower is better so your equipment is always at it's best.
Satisfying core gameplay loop of fishing and running the restaurant, broken up by a fun set of missions with an absolutely WILD amount of different gameplay mechanics sprinkled throughout.

I initially expected Dave the Diver to be a straightforward game about catching fish and running a sushi restaurant. However, it is so much deeper than that, and the references throughout the game are excellent.
Aku menikmati game ini. permainan yang simpel dan bagus, cocok untuk santai dan banyak sekali mini game-mini game yang seru
I have not played much yet but so far it's very fun and i would recommend it for the art alone
cute game, lots of different things to do, funny cutscenes
Does a lot with the time you are invested in it. Minigames, sub-missions, etc are all fun in their own way. Solid game all around

absolute peak
so much content for a 2d pixel game is crazy
I could recommend this to everyone and for 20 bucks it's definitely worth it

Genuinely amazed. I've seen the overwhelmingly positive rating for a couple years, but always looked at the game and was confused about what it was about. That's the magic. One minute your fighting a shark, the next your dancing in the audience at a concert, the next your farming, and the next your taking picture of a giant manta ray or riding a boluga. But it all makes sense, and its all done with a fun gameplay loop that even when it should be repetitive, doesn't feel repetitive. 

Highly recommend.
